task_updates.executor_notes: Added `gpu_orchestrator/control/capacity_reconciler.py` observation support and extended `ObservedPoolState` with excluded-worker and route-demand provenance fields. Observation reads workers and task counts once, preserves the `partition_capacity_workers()` boundary by excluding live-test/harness workers from capacity derivation while returning them in `excluded_workers`, derives selected-pool route demand from exact `route_totals`, safely infers single-route selected totals, falls back to an explicit `__unattributed_route__` demand instead of `__pool_floor__` when route attribution is ambiguous, applies route-scoped backoff reads, derives worker snapshots from production workers only, and counts only included non-terminal non-route-stale active/spawning workers as effective capacity. Added `tests/gpu_orchestrator/control/test_capacity_reconciler_observe.py` covering excluded workers, exact/inferred/fallback route demand, worker metadata route attribution, route backoff suppression, and route-stale effective-capacity exclusion. Verified with focused observation tests, the full `tests/gpu_orchestrator/control` module, route-aware task count tests, and py_compile. Full suite was run and failed only at the already observed missing `scripts.spot_instance_lifetime_test` import, unrelated to T4.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Observation partitions live-test/harness workers out before derivation and capacity counts while returning them through `excluded_workers` for cleanup visibility. Route demand handles exact selected `route_totals`, safe single-route inference from selected-pool totals, explicit unattributed fallback for ambiguous selected totals, and route-scoped backoff reads without using `__pool_floor__` for observed demand.
